epic.name: Alert Routing Rules
epic.objective: Implement configurable routing rules that direct alerts to specific Slack channels, teams, or incident commanders based on alert labels, severity, and source metadata
epic.definition_of_done: Alerts are automatically routed to the correct Slack channel and/or assigned to the correct team based on matching routing rules. Rules are configurable via API and UI. Unmatched alerts use default behavior.
epic.notes: Routing rules determine WHERE an incident is handled (which channel, which team). This is distinct from grouping rules (which alerts belong together). Routing runs before grouping in the pipeline: Normalize → Deduplicate → Route → Group → Create Incident.

tasks.name: Design routing rules data model
tasks.implementation_notes: RoutingRule model with JSONB match_criteria and actions. Migration 000009. RoutingRuleRepository with CRUD + CheckPriorityConflict. Default seed rule at priority 1000 (create incidents for critical/warning). Partial index on priority WHERE enabled=true.

tasks.name: Implement routing rules evaluation engine
tasks.implementation_notes: RoutingEngine with EvaluateAlert + RefreshRules. RWMutex cache (5s TTL) with double-checked locking to prevent thundering herd. First-match-wins over priority-ordered enabled rules. match_criteria: source list, severity list, labels map with * wildcard (AND semantics). RoutingDecision: Suppress, SeverityOverride, ChannelOverride, RuleName.

tasks.name: Integrate routing engine into alert processing pipeline
tasks.implementation_notes: Wired in ProcessNormalizedAlerts: Deduplicate → Route → [suppress?] → [severity_override in-memory] → ShouldCreateIncident → Group → Create/Link. Suppress skips incident creation entirely. severity_override applied to in-memory alert only (DB record preserves original monitoring-tool severity for audit).

tasks.name: Routing rules CRUD API
tasks.implementation_notes: GET/POST /api/v1/routing-rules, GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/routing-rules/:id. PATCH (not PUT) to match frontend ApiClient. onRuleMutate callback invalidates engine cache on every mutation. Priority uniqueness enforced via CheckPriorityConflict. Shared isNotFound() helper extracted to handlers/helpers.go.

tasks.name: Routing rules management UI
tasks.implementation_notes: RoutingRulesPage with full CRUD table + inline RuleModal. Columns: priority, name/description, match criteria summary, action summary, enabled badge, edit/delete. JSON textarea inputs for match_criteria and actions with client-side validation. GitFork nav item in Sidebar. Route wired in App.tsx.

tasks.name: Integration tests for routing rules
tasks.implementation_notes: 12 unit tests in routing_engine_test.go using mockRoutingRuleRepo. Covers: no-rules default, suppress match/non-match, severity override, channel override, wildcard label, exact label, priority order (first-match-wins), empty criteria matches all, multi-criteria AND semantics, repo error, cache refresh.

internal: Alert grouping engine (OI-EPIC-013, optional — routing works without grouping)
